about.description: I'm Adriel, a product designer and trainer. My mission: simplify SaaS creation, inspire, and guide those who want to bring their ideas to life.
about.keywords: about Adriel Zimbril, SaaS Product Designer, SaaS trainer, SaaS product design journey, ShiroSaaS method

craft-section.description: Centering the user in every decision, guiding and teaching creators to turn every idea into unforgettable experiences.
craft-section.title: Transform ideas into meaningful products.

header-section.description: Since my beginnings in design, I learned that the most successful product is not the one you see first, but the one you understand, test, and evolve intentionally.
header-section.description-secondary:
Sic itur ad astra — that’s how we reach the stars 🫰🏻✨.

Since I was young, I’ve been fascinated by technology and invention. Iron Man sparked my taste for possibility: I remember my eyes sparkling when I saw his armor and the inspiration flowing through each episode. I spent hours imagining, tinkering, and dreaming of creating my own robots.

Following this passion, I discovered design and over time, I realized my dream was not just to build machines: I wanted to create experiences that touch people, solve real problems, and make people want to use them. Design became my playground, my laboratory to imagine, build, and transform raw ideas into concrete, impactful products.

Today, I turn ideas into tangible experiences, helping founders, teams, and creators clarify their problems, imagine solutions, and launch quickly. My approach combines thoughtful design, agile strategy, and continuous learning to make each project truly impactful.

I love questioning what exists, exploring what works and what doesn’t, and building intentionally. I prefer to do things with a purpose rather than out of habit.

Meanwhile, I share methods and resources from my creative experiences so others can learn to design and launch products confidently.

For me, design is not an isolated deliverable: it’s a collaborative, creative, and human process ❣️.
header-section.title:
Hello, I am 👋 
 Adriel Zimbril 🐼 
 Product Designer & Trainer 🎨
header-section.title-secondary:
Create, simplify, inspire 🌒🧑🏻‍🚀🫶🏻 
 Welcome to my universe 🌌

content.conclusion: Facts, figures, crushing failures, and unexpected victories. Design as it really happens ❤️.

content.item2: User feedback that made me change my mind
content.item3: My mistakes and how I try to avoid them
content.subtitle: My observations on:
content.title: In this newsletter, I simply share what I learn.

cta.description: A guide on common UX mistakes I often see in SaaS. Practical observations I’ve gathered from my projects.
cta.note: And of course, you can unsubscribe whenever you want. I only write when I have something useful to share.
cta.title: I’ve prepared something to get started.

newsletterSignup.preview: Hi! I wanted to tell you about something close to my heart...

story.intro: A few weeks ago, a user of one of my apps wrote to tell me they took a long time to cancel an action. I realized it was due to a design choice I liked… but which complicated the experience.
story.philosophy: What really interests me is understanding how people use what we create. Why they give up at certain points, why they come back, and what really helps them in their daily lives.
story.presentation: My name is Adriel, and for 5 years I’ve been creating interfaces for SaaS products and I noticed something: we often complicate things when users are just looking for simplicity.

newsletterSignup.subject: 🎁 Receive the gifts
newsletterSignup.tagline: Problem Solver & Your SaaS Product Designer 🦄

why.motivation: I want to share these little discoveries, these moments when you think "oh, I wouldn’t have thought of that". No pretension, just shared curiosity ❤️.
why.reason: Because I like to understand how things work. And in design, there’s often a gap between what we think will work and what actually works.
why.title: Why do I really write?

productDelivery.message1: I’m glad you’re part of this adventure. I hope this resource helps you go further, faster.
productDelivery.message2: Your feedback really helps me improve my content for the next ones. And if you have questions along the way, don’t hesitate to ask!
productDelivery.preview: Your {productType} is ready to be downloaded!

course.feedback: After completing some modules, I’d love your feedback. Does the content meet your expectations? Are there points you want to explore further?
course.tip: 💡 Tip: take notes while following the modules, it really helps to remember and apply.

ebook.feedback: When you’ve had time to go through it, I’d love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What still raises questions?
ebook.tip: 📖 Suggestion: read it in small sections and try to apply what you learn directly.

video.feedback: Once you’ve watched the masterclass, tell me what you think! Does it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
video.tip: 🎯 Tip: watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while listening.

productDelivery.subject: 🎁 Receive the gifts

header-section.description: Every project is an adventure. Here I share behind the scenes: challenges, solutions, and results.
header-section.title: Case studies 🚀

goal-research-section.description: From this problem, we draw the project compass: goals and indicators to show if we’re on the right track.
goal-research-section.title: Objective?

project-points-research-section.description: Every data point and feedback becomes lessons that illuminate design choices and guide our decisions.
project-points-research-section.title: Insights

project-preview-section.description: Each screen tells a story: how users discover the product, interact with it, and experience it in real context.
project-preview-section.title: Preview

project-research-section.description: It all starts with the problem: who is affected, in what context, and why it deserves our attention.
project-research-section.title: Problem framing

project-result-section.description: The project comes to life and tells its success story through adoption, retention, and impact on users and business.
project-result-section.title: Results

project-statement-section.description: User needs and behaviors emerge through interviews, observations, and exchanges, revealing their needs, frustrations, and motivations.
project-statement-section.title: User research
